message:
fix: harden mux lifecycle against leak classes in subscribers, tab state, and tmux queue

- Wrap subscriber callbacks in catch_unwind to prevent panicking subscribers
  from leaking in the notification map (mux/lib.rs)
- Remove dead pane IDs from collapsed_panes on pane removal (mux/tab.rs)
- Add CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH (50k) hard cap on tmux command queue with
  oldest-first drain to prevent unbounded growth during event storms (mux/tmux.rs)
- Remove duplicate tcp_tls bench entry in frankenterm-core Cargo.toml
- Add regression tests for subscriber panic safety and queue cap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ repository.workspace = true
 readme = "../../README.md"
 keywords = ["terminal", "frankenterm", "automation", "ai-agents"]
 categories = ["development-tools"]
+autobenches = false
 
 [lints]
 workspace = true
@@ -117,6 +118,7 @@ proptest = "1.8.0"
 tempfile = "3.23.0"
 criterion = { version = "0.7.0", features = ["async_tokio"] }
 loom = "0.7"
+rcgen = { workspace = true }
 
 # Benchmarks
 [[bench]]
@@ -172,6 +174,11 @@ name = "task_spawn"
 harness = false
 required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "tcp_tls"
+harness = false
+required-features = ["asupersync-runtime", "distributed"]
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "sizing_benchmark"
 harness = false
